turns.content: I'm trying to use #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] on a struct but I keep getting errors when my field names don't match the JSON keys. How do I map them correctly?

That's a common stumbling block when you return to coding after a break. The core idea is that serde needs to know how your Rust fields correspond to the JSON keys, much like translating between two different languages requires a dictionary. You can use attribute annotations on your struct or individual fields to tell serde which JSON key should map to which Rust field name. For example, #[serde(rename = "user_name")] tells serde to look for that specific string in the incoming data and assign it to your user_name field.
Oh, so if I just add those attributes everywhere, does that handle everything automatically? What about when a JSON object has extra fields or missing ones?

Exactly, once you've added the rename attributes, serde will use them as a lookup table to match keys. For missing fields in the JSON, you can skip them entirely by adding #[serde(skip)] to that field definition so it gets ignored during deserialization. Conversely, if your Rust struct has default values for optional fields, you can tell serde to treat those specific fields as having defaults using #[serde(default)] which makes them always present even when absent in the input data.
